edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:78e45b679345
edges.original_sentence: The water table level (lower than 40 cm below the soil surface) and frost heaving appear to be the main factors explaining the failure of cotton-grass and of other typical bog plant species to colonize abandoned mined surfaces.

edges.original_sentence: Frost heaving is the most probable cause of uprooting, and this may explain why so few new tussocks established

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:78e45b679345
edges.original_sentence: Frost heaving and the high peat oxidation rate measured in peatlands of the Bas-Saint-Laurent region also probably explain, in part, the high mortality rate of small trees

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:5eb252a97112
edges.original_sentence: Even a slight increase in the water table level seems to improve growth conditions for many bog species, as observed at the RDLP2 site.

edges.original_sentence: In Europe, flooding of mined bogs has been used to accelerate the spontaneous re-establishment of vascular plants and of some Sphagnum species

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:43528bfd0c84
edges.original_sentence: Alternative restoration procedures, such as the spreading of Sphagnum diaspores combined with the use of straw mulch to protect diaspores from desiccation, and of fertilizers to accelerate the growth of companion plants ... represent the best management option to rapidly re-establish a plant cover
